mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 6.0 percent, labor market slack dissipates, boosting consumer confidence and aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat forward returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms a broad-based economic expansion, reducing equity risk premiums and lifting S&P 500 valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5 percent over 60-day windows in more than 60 percent of rolling 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding trend signals robust corporate capacity utilization and pricing power, which translates into higher earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO growth decelerates for two consecutive months, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52 percent in a 10-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation rising above 2.5 percent annually forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of high-inflation episodes, or if the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.01 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth accelerating above 2.0 percent while the unemployment rate remains below 6.5 percent indicates a self-reinforcing growth cycle that sustains equity risk appetite and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year rolling window, or forward returns under the joint condition do not exceed the baseline by at least 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction spending reflects improved household balance sheets and credit availability, which directly supports consumer-driven corporate revenues and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50 percent of observations when RSAFS declines, or the rolling 1-year Rank IC remains below 0.025.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls growth consistently exceeding 300,000 per month for three consecutive periods signals labor market overheating, triggering wage-price spiral fears that compress equity valuations and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS level exceeds 134000 for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of overheating episodes, or if the predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.
